_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index comprising average prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice set to 100 in 2015, stands at 141, indicating a 41% rise over the period. This increase is uneven among components and largely reflects recent upward price movements rather than a steady climb. The index serves as a snapshot of key soft commodity costs used for breakfast products globally. These figures are recorded from World commodity price data compiled by FRED and IMF sources.
notes.movers: The main contributors to the basket's rise are coffee and orange juice, with coffee prices nearly doubling since 2015 (up 99%) and orange juice rising by 34%. Wheat and sugar have increased more modestly by 19% and 13%, respectively. These price changes are driven by specific supply shocks, notably weather events and crop diseases like citrus greening and adverse frost or drought in arabica coffee regions. Soft commodity markets are relatively thin, meaning lower liquidity and greater sensitivity to such disruptions, which amplifies price volatility.
notes.why: The headline price gains in the breakfast basket are primarily due to a few weather-sensitive crops experiencing supply constraints. These conditions reflect localized and sporadic factors rather than broad-based inflationary pressures. Consequently, input costs for commodities like coffee and orange juice can remain elevated for extended periods, depending on the persistence of these environmental challenges.
